# orcasecurity_sensitive_data_identifier (Resource)

Provides a custom DSPM sensitive data identifier (detector). Sensitive data identifiers describe the data patterns DSPM scanning looks for.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# custom sensitive data identifier matching 9-digit account numbers
resource "orcasecurity_sensitive_data_identifier" "account_number" {
  title        = "Internal Account Number"
  details      = "Detects internal 9-digit account numbers."
  category     = "PII"
  sub_category = "Financial"
  properties = {
    conditions = [
      { value = "ACC-[0-9]{9}" }
    ]
    detection_types = ["text", "db"]
    sensitivity     = "high"
    significance    = "major"
    keywords        = ["account", "acct"]
    text_threshold  = 2
  }
}

# disabled identifier with default detection types
resource "orcasecurity_sensitive_data_identifier" "legacy_token" {
  title        = "Legacy API Token"
  details      = "Detects legacy API token format."
  category     = "SECRET"
  sub_category = "Credentials"
  enabled      = false
  properties = {
    conditions = [
      { value = "legacy_[a-f0-9]{32}" }
    ]
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `category` (String) Data category. Valid values are `PII`, `PHI`, `PCI`, `SECRET`, and `OTHER`.
- `details` (String) Identifier description.
- `properties` (Attributes) Detection configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--properties))
- `sub_category` (String) Free-form sub-category (e.g. `Personal`, `Medical`).
- `title` (String) Identifier title. Must be unique within the organization.

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the identifier is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Sensitive data identifier ID.
- `organization_id` (String) Orca organization ID.

<a id="nestedatt--properties"></a>
### Nested Schema for `properties`

Required:

- `conditions` (Attributes List) Content-matching conditions. At least one is required. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--properties--conditions))

Optional:

- `ai_threshold` (Number) Minimum matches for AI detection.
- `db_threshold` (Number) Minimum matches for database detection.
- `detection_types` (List of String) Detection engines to run. Valid values are `text`, `db`, `ocr`, and `ai`. Defaults to the server default (`["text", "db"]`).
- `exclude_keywords` (List of String) Keywords that suppress detection.
- `keywords` (List of String) Keywords that boost detection confidence.
- `ocr_threshold` (Number) Minimum matches for OCR detection.
- `sensitivity` (String) Data sensitivity. Valid values are `critical`, `high`, `medium`, and `low`.
- `significance` (String) Finding significance. Valid values are `minor`, `moderate`, and `major`.
- `stop_wildcards` (List of String) Path wildcards excluded from scanning.
- `text_threshold` (Number) Minimum matches for text detection.

<a id="nestedatt--properties--conditions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `properties.conditions`

Required:

- `value` (String) Regular expression to match.

Optional:

- `operator` (String) Condition operator. Defaults to `match`.
- `source` (String) Condition source. Defaults to `content`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import orcasecurity_sensitive_data_identifier.example IDENTIFIER_ID
```
